samples.xml_description:
<module id="calendar">
    <description>Implement a perpetual calendar.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK">Clock input.</input>
        <input id="RST">Active high reset signal.</input>
        <output id="Hours" width_description="[5:0]">Current hours.</output>
        <output id="Mins" width_description="[5:0]">Current minutes.</output>
        <output id="Secs" width_description="[5:0]">Current seconds.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="Secs">Seconds value.</parameter>
        <parameter id="Mins">Minutes value.</parameter>
        <parameter id="Hours">Hours value.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        Always block for updating the seconds value based on the clock and reset signals.
        If the reset signal is active, set the seconds to 0.
        If the seconds value is 59, wrap around and set the seconds to 0.
        Otherwise, increment the seconds by 1.
        Always block for updating the minutes value based on the clock and reset signals.
        If the reset signal is active, set the minutes to 0.
        If both the minutes and seconds values are 59, wrap around and set the minutes to 0.
        If the seconds value is 59, increment the minutes by 1.
        Otherwise, keep the minutes value unchanged.
        Always block for updating the hours value based on the clock and reset signals.
        If the reset signal is active, set the hours to 0.
        If the hours, minutes, and seconds values are all at their maximum, wrap around and set the hours to 0.
        If the minutes and seconds values are both 59, increment the hours by 1.
        Otherwise, keep the hours value unchanged.
    </implementation>
</module>
